title: RSA private key stored plaintext in SQLite (no at-rest encryption)
description:
LOCATION: src/db.rs:14 (schema: private_key TEXT NOT NULL), src/persona.rs:57

FINDING: The RSA private key PEM for each persona is stored as plaintext TEXT in the personas table of the SQLite database. The database file has a runtime file-permission warning (src/server.rs:91-103) but no encryption. Any backup of the database file, any read access to the file, or any SQL injection vulnerability anywhere in the app grants full signing capability for all personas.

IMPACT: Theft of the SQLite file (e.g., via backup misconfiguration, sysadmin error, or local file read vulnerability) immediately yields all private signing keys. The attacker can impersonate any persona on the federated network and sign arbitrary ActivityPub activities on their behalf.
close_reason: Accepted: RSA private key must be readable for signing. Encryption at rest requires a key management system. ponytail: upgrade to HSM/KMS when available.
